name: Chime Financial, Inc.
one_liner: Chime is a US neobank offering fee-free mobile checking, savings, credit-building, and earned-wage-access products primarily to everyday Americans underserved by traditional banks.

ambiguities: Total private funding reported varies by source: Wikipedia/CNBC cite $2.3B; Tracxn cites $3.15B (likely includes all round tranches); $2.3B is the most-cited figure pre-IPO but Tracxn's $3.15B may capture additional secondary/debt rounds. Used $3.15B (Tracxn) as the more comprehensive figure.
Chime went public on NASDAQ (ticker CHYM) in June 2025, raising $864M at an $18.4B valuation per Wikipedia; the last_round field is set to null as is standard for public companies.

Headcount figures vary: MacroTrends/SEC filings show 1,519 (FY2025 annual); Unify (Oct 2025 estimate) shows ~1,796; LeadIQ (Apr 2026) shows ~2,400. Used SEC/MacroTrends figure of 1,519 as most authoritative (annual report basis).
business_model set to 'B2C marketplace' as closest fit; Chime is primarily a B2C consumer fintech platform monetized via interchange fees — no single schema option perfectly captures this model.
